_schema.description: Humanitarian response capability scores across six variables for three regions. Fictional placeholder with realistic distributional shape — designed to produce distinct polygon profiles that illustrate the chart's strength. Proves the radar renders before real data is substituted.

fields.variables[].key: string — field key referenced in series[].values
fields.variables[].label: string — axis label displayed on chart (≤14 chars)
fields.variables[].description: string — fuller definition used in tooltip
fields.scale.min: number — minimum value across all axes (always 0 for radar)
fields.scale.max: number — maximum value across all axes (axes share one scale)
fields.series[].label: string — series name for legend
fields.series[].color: string — hex color from/derived from hai palette
fields.series[].values: object — keys must match variables[].key; values 0–scale.max

_schema.replace: Replace variables[] with your dimensions (keep 4–8 for readability). Replace series[] with your entities. All series share the same scale — normalise your values to scale.max before substituting.

variables.label: Access
variables.description: Humanitarian access to affected populations (0 = no access, 100 = full access)

variables.label: Capacity
variables.description: Operational and human resource capacity index

variables.label: Coverage
variables.description: Percentage of people in need reached with assistance

variables.label: Speed
variables.description: Response speed index — higher = faster first delivery
